title: TC001-Browse live trade feed and open a session detail
description: Verifies a guest can load the home feed with session cards, status and stats, then open a session detail view from the feed.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Session Details')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The session detail view should be visible after clicking a session card in the feed.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Audit Timeline')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The 5-step audit timeline should be visible in the session detail view."]} PMID: 0/4. title: TC002-Guest can monitor trade feed and open a session detail
description: Verifies that a guest can load the home feed and navigate into a session detail view successfully.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Session Details')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The session detail view should be visible after clicking a session card.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '5-step audit timeline')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The 5-step audit timeline should be visible in the session detail view."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Run on-chain verification and view per-step results
description: Verifies a user can initiate on-chain verification for a session and see per-step verification result badges displayed.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Reload the page (perform a navigation to the same URL) to try to recover the SPA rendering.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Reload the app using http://127.0.0.1:3000/ and wait 5 seconds for the SPA to render and populate interactive elements.
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:3000/")
        
        # -> Navigate to the backend API at http://64.227.139.172:4000/ and wait to see if the service is reachable (helps determine whether the app being blank is server-side).
        await page.goto("http://64.227.139.172:4000/")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Verified')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Per-step verification result badges should be visible after initiating on-chain verification for the session",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Verification complete')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "An overall verification status should be displayed after on-chain verification finishes for the session"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The feature could not be reached — the app UI did not load and the backend responded with an error, so the verification flow cannot be tested.

Observations:
- The home feed page showed no interactive elements (blank / SPA did not render) after multiple navigations and waits.
- The backend API root (http://64.227.139.172:4000/) returned {"error":"not found"} when accessed.

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Try loading the feed via a different route (navigate to /sessions) so the SPA boot process can be retried and then wait for the feed to finish loading.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/sessions")
        
        # -> Click the top navigation 'feed' link to load the feed (element index 1413).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/header/div/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Feed')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The trade feed should be displayed after navigating to the feed.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Sessions')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The feed should show session cards so guests can browse sessions.",
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC013-Wallet connect is accessible from non-home pages and persists when navigating
description: Verifies that connecting a wallet from another page persists and is still shown when returning to home.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /analytics (use the explicit navigate action to http://localhost:3000/analytics), then observe the page for interactive elements and the wallet connect UI.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/analytics")
        
        # -> Click the 'connect wallet' button (index 679) to open the wallet modal (do not perform a real wallet extension flow). Then return to the home page by clicking 'feed' (index 604) and observe whether a connected address is shown.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/header/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/header/div/div/nav/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '0x1234...abcd')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The connected wallet address should be visible in the navigation after returning to home.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'INIT')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The INIT balance card should be visible on the home page after returning from analytics.",
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC014-Disconnecting wallet returns UI to guest state on home
description: Verifies that a connected user can disconnect and the UI returns to the guest state without showing address/balance.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Connect Wallet')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The navigation should show Connect Wallet after disconnect.",
        assert not await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'INIT')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The INIT balance card should not be visible after disconnect."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
